nameMeanings.name: Rodney
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name from a lost or as yet unidentified place, perhaps near Markham in Somerset, and apparently named from the Old English personal name Hrōda (a short form of various compound names with the first element hrōth ‘renown’) + ēg ‘island, raised area in marsh’. The use of Rodney as a personal name probably developed in the 18th century. Compare Radney .

variants.name: Radney
meanings: English: perhaps a habitational name from Radnage in Buckinghamshire, named from Old English rēad ‘red’ + ǣc, dative of āc ‘oak’. There are only a few occurrences of this surname in England.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: Richard Rodney was born in 1509, in Rodney Stoke, Somerset, England, United Kingdom. He had at least 2 daughters with Jane Schuckburgh. He died in 1565, in England, United Kingdom, at the age of 56, and was buried in Upper Shuckburgh, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard Rodneyは1509年にイギリスイギリスソメルセトロドニー ストクで生まれた。彼はJane Schuckburghとの間に少なくとも2人の娘をもうけた。彼は1565年にイギリスイギリスで56歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスワーウィクシュアイアウップエル シュクブルグにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Rodney(은)는 1509년 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 로드니 스토크에서 태어났다. 그는 Jane Schuckburgh(와)과 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1565년 영국 잉글랜드에서 56세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르 업퍼 슈ck버그h에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Richard Rodney родился в 1509 г. в местности родни сток, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания. У него было по крайней мере 2 дочери от Jane Schuckburgh. Он умер в 1565 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 56 лет и был похоронен в местности уппир шуккбург, варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания.
